title: rollback event on the bus + pre-error snapshot
manager: When a macrostep rolls back after a violation, it emits a rollback event carrying a snapshot of the pre-error state, so observers can see exactly what was attempted and undone. Rollback becomes an observable, first-class event rather than a silent revert — which is what makes contract failures debuggable, and what lets a supervisor or logger react to a failed transaction instead of never learning it happened.
cs: rollback event on the observation bus with a pre-error snapshot payload; fires on macrostep reverse-replay (W6.69); consumed through the hook registry (W6.77).
